title: Print a Phone Stand
description: Practice 3D printing by making a handy phone stand.

dialogue.text: Hey, Sydney again! People keep propping phones on toolboxes in the co-op, so let's make a stand that looks clean and actually holds.
First: safety and surface. Ventilate the room, tie hair back, and keep sleeves clear of the gantry. Wipe the bed with 90% isopropyl so the first layer sticks, then check the nozzle is clear.

options.text: Swap to white PLA and purge.
Bed is clean and filament is loaded.

dialogue.text: Grab the phone stand STL from the community library. Check your phone width and case thickness; we want a 2-3 mm lip so it doesn't slide. If your slicer supports scaling, scale the model to fit your phone width plus 2 mm of clearance.

options.text: Model looks good. Let's slice it.

dialogue.text: Slice for strength: 0.2 mm layers, 3 perimeters, 4 top/bottom layers, and 15% gyroid infill. Use a 3-5 mm brim if your corners lift. Supports are only needed if the overhang exceeds ~60°.

options.text: Slicer settings set. Ready to print.

dialogue.text: Preheat to your PLA profile (around 200°C hotend, 60°C bed). Watch the first layer: you want a smooth, slightly squished line with no gaps. Let it run without drafts or bumps.

Let it cool fully, then flex the bed to release. Trim any stringing with a hobby knife. Test fit your phone: it should rest at a 15-20° angle and stay put when you tap the screen. Add a tiny felt pad if it slides.
Nice work! Power down the printer, log your settings for next time, and drop a photo in the co-op channel so others can remix the design.
